title: Ukraine's Zelenskiy says he spoke to Trump, calls for 'American resolve' to help end war
gist: Zelenskiy says a July 4 call with Trump on the front-line situation was 'very good' and that 'American resolve' is now the crucial factor in ending the more-than-four-year war with Russia, with the two agreeing to continue the conversation at next week's NATO summit in Turkey.

insights.claim: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said he spoke with US President Donald Trump on July 4, 2026, describing the conversation about the front-line situation as 'very good.'
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: A direct, positively-characterized Trump-Zelenskiy call is itself a signal worth tracking for continued US engagement on the Ukraine file.
insights.locator: Zelenskiy said he had a ‘very good’ conversation

insights.claim: Zelenskiy said there is 'a real prospect' to end the war with Russia and that 'American resolve will have a crucial meaning' in achieving it.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Zelenskiy is explicitly pinning the war's endgame on the depth of US commitment -- a framing to watch against actual US policy moves in the following weeks.
insights.quote: There is a real prospect to end this war and American resolve will have a crucial meaning

insights.claim: Zelenskiy and Trump agreed to continue their conversation at next week's NATO summit in Turkey.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Flags a specific near-term event (the NATO summit in Turkey) as the next checkpoint for any movement on a Ukraine war settlement.
insights.locator: Zelenskiy said he and Trump had agreed
